lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Hello there. The name's Vanstrom Klause. How goes it stranger?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quite well, thanks for asking. How about you?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Hmm, well, I am a little concerned about some friends of mine. They're in dire need of some assistance, but I'm at a loss as to how I can help them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What friends are these?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: It's a personal tragedy that I have yet to meet them in the flesh. But their exploits make mouth watering stories... The real meat and drink of high adventure and daring... So they say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does that mean exactly? I mean, I have some stories. I'm quite a hero myself, you may actually be talking about me?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: They're a group of rebels fighting against the vampyres that rule over Morytania. They're known as the Myreque. Some people call them terrorists while others call them freedom fighters!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. And you want to help them?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Of course. I'm a big fan of their cause. I was hoping to supply them with some new weapons. Alas, I find that I'm rather lacking in ability when it comes to this kind of thing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well perhaps I could take these weapons to the Myreque. What do they need?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Really? You would help?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I was hoping to deliver some steel to them. A longsword, two shortswords, a dagger, a mace and a warhammer.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Though if you wish to do this, I'm afraid you'll need to acquire the weapons yourself. My efforts to procure them have been rather unsuccessful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Get some steel weapons and take them to the Myreque. Where can I find them?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: The vampyres are always hunting them, so they keep themselves hidden. I hear the boatman in Mort'ton has dealings with them though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'd better get going then.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: How wonderful! Perhaps the people of Morytania now have an additional hero that they can come to rely on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What else do you know about the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: There's always some evil heel ready to grind the face of humanity into the dirt. Here in Morytania, it's the vampyres! They rule over the region with an iron fist, killing any who oppose them.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: The Myreque are the only ones brave enough to stand up to them. They stay in the shadows though, lest the vampyres track them down!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: No one knows for sure how many of them there are. The group hiding in Mort Myre supposedly has six members. There are whispers of more in other parts of Morytania though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And this group in Mort Myre? They're who the weapons are for?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Yes, indeed! The Myreque are almost certainly able to handle themselves... But they need the tools to do it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remind me, what weapons do I need?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: You've forgotten already? Ha! That's funny! But I do understand, my memory isn't what it was either!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I'd suggest a longsword, two shortswords, a dagger, a mace and a warhammer, all made of steel.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: You'll need to obtain the weapons yourself. Fear not though! If you return to me here once you've found them, I'll be sure to give you a nice surprise in return for your help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find this boatman?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I hear he lives in Mort'ton. You'll find the town south of here, through Mort Myre. It's a nasty place, but where isn't in Morytania?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Most of the town has supposedly fallen to some strange affliction. The boatman is apparently one of the few healthy people left!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you say he's had dealings with the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: So I hear. There are many who sympathise with their movement. Rumour is that this boatman's been ferrying supplies to their hideout, somewhere in Mort Myre!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better be off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wish I could help, but I'm busy at the moment.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Oh well, I'm sure someone will be along to help soon. Thanks for even considering it though.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Hello again. Any luck finding the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Hello there friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'm guessing you're the boatman?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Well I am 'a' boatman. I don't know about 'the' boatman though. Why do you ask anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you have dealings with the Myreque. I was hoping to take some weapons to them.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Hmm, I'm sure I don't know what you're talking about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Come on, I know you're in cahoots with them. It's important that I get these weapons to them.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Well... Okay, I may have done some work for them before. It's too dangerous now though. I'm sorry, I can't help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh come on, you can tell me!

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: I'm sorry, I just can't... They're watching us you know... Eyes everywhere!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I guess they'll just die without weapons.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Hmm, you don't seem too concerned about their welfare. I'm glad I didn't tell you where they are. In any case, they're resourceful, they can look after themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that supposed to mean?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: They're resourceful folks, that's all I'm saying. The leader of their group, Veliaf, looks after them well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Resourceful enough to get their own steel weapons?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Maybe they are... What do you care anyway? They've been up against it ever since they got started.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: All of 'em have suffered more loss and heartache than you'll ever know. Now, leave me be!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have they been up against?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: You're clearly not from around here or you wouldn't be asking such foolish questions.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Drakan. That's what they've been up against.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Drakan?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Lord Lowerniel Drakan. Leader of the vampyres and ruler of Morytania. His reign over these lands means we all live in fear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of loss and heartache have they suffered?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: The worst kind. Most have lost members of their family.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Take Sani Piliu for example. she was orphaned overnight when a vampyre went on the rampage. Imagine that, losing your entire family in one night. Terrible!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds awful... Who is Sani Piliu?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: She's the only female member of the Myreque. Her agility and light fingers have got them out of quite a few sticky situations.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you don't tell me, their deaths are on your head!

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: There's death aplenty in this forsaken place... What do I care that some fool hardy vigilantes decided to go it alone against the Drakans?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Stupidity of youth is to blame. I won't carry it on my shoulders!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One man's vigilante is another man's freedom fighter!

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Aye, you can see it from both sides I suppose. But many of us consider it fool hardy to fight for something we'll never get.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: I remember a conversation I had with Polmafi, a scholar, such as he was. Even he agrees that the chances are slim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Polmafi? Who's he?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Polmafi Ferdygris is one of the Myreque. He's a technical sort and advises on all sorts of things to Veliaf. He was a scholar before he became a renegade.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know of the Drakans?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: They're the family that leads the vampyres. They're the ones to whom the blood tithes are paid. Too much I have told you already!

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Ignorance is better than these truths I tell you! I can pretend once more that I am a free man and some relief from this gloom can I feel again.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Be gone with you now and leave me with my dreams.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of a man are you to say that you don't care?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Don't dare to judge me young fool... what do you know of the heartache I carry? I want to help them! I did help them! But my part is done now. To do any more is to risk death!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're risking death either way. Better to risk it and help the cause, than to stand by and do nothing.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Hmmm. Very well, if you would take your chance to help these strangers, who am I to stop you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll help me? Will you take me to them?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: No, I won't take you, but you can use my boat.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: You'll be going through Mort Myre though, so I won't be letting you go unless you've got some defence against the ghasts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have anything which I can use against them.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Well return to me once you have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this druid pouch! This turns the ghasts visible and I can kill them once I can see them.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Very well, you can go! But you'll need to bring me six wooden planks first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What for?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: The boat took some damage on my last trip. It needs repairing. There's also a bridge you'll need to cross later in your journey. It's rotten and will likely need repairs.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Three of the planks are for the boat and three are for the bridge. I suggest you bring some tools and steel nails along as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here are some planks for you.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Right. You can take the boat. Just jump in when you're ready to leave.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Go as far north as you can and you'll arrive at the Hollows. Keep heading north on foot, over the rotten bridge, and you'll eventually reach an odd looking tree. That is where you'll find the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you say that this place is forsaken?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: All of these lands are forsaken of Saradomin's kindness, only cold death from evil do we now feel.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Those lucky ones to the west of the Salve little realise their fate if the river should one day become tainted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll give you some cash if you tell me.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: You think you can buy me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, no. I just want to compensate you for your trouble.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: You keep your money and I'll keep my secrets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just want to help them, I think they need help.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Aye, everyone in these lands needs help. I'm sorry though, it's just not safe and it's not likely to get safer any time soon.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Look, I feel for them, I really do. They're fighting the good fight out there and unfortunately, some of them probably won't live through it.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Ivan in particular I feel sorry for. He's the youngest of their group and he's seen too few winters to be involved in such toil.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then tell me how to find them. I can help them.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: I support their cause. That doesn't mean I want to die for them though. I'm sorry, but I will not risk the wrath of the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please. I need to know how to find the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: I'm sorry, but I can't help you. I guess you're all out of luck.

sections.title: Talking to Cyreg after convincing him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, will you take me to the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: No, I won't take you, but you can use my boat.

sections.title: Talking to Cyreg after fixing the boaty

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I ask you some more questions?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Sure you can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where am I meant to be going?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Take my boat as far north as you can and you'll arrive at the Hollows. Keep heading north on foot, over the rotten bridge, and you'll eventually reach an odd looking tree. That is where you'll find the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: What do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are they called the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: It's a name the locals gave them. It means 'hidden in the myre'. Nothing fancy, it's just because that's where they hide, in Mort Myre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the members of the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Well you have Radigad Ponfit. He was a mercenary from Asgarnia but he joined up with the Myreque to settle some mysterious score with the Drakans.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Then there's Veliaf Hurtz, the leader of the group. He's been fighting the fight longer than any of them.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Next there's Ivan Strom. He's the youngest. I hear he's an aspiring priest. Not that there's much time for that around here.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: You've also got Sani Piliu, the only female of the group. She's a bit of a shady one. I reckon she was a thief before the Myreque found her.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: There's Harold Evans. Used to be part of some militia but was kicked out for being too hot-headed. Now he's the main fighter for the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: Finally, you have Polmafi Ferdygris. He's the brains of the operation in many ways. Believe it or not, he used to be a scholar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that's all of them?

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: In Mort Myre it is. I sometimes hear rumour of more groups hidden in other parts of Morytania. Who knows if that's true though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Drakans.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: They're the family of vampyres that rules all of Morytania. Lowerniel Drakan is the head of the family. Every bit of misery in this place is caused by him.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: No one out here ever sees the Drakans, but their presence is always felt. Their castle towers over all of the region. A constant reminder of their tyranny.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Cyreg Paddlehorn
lines.text: I'm just a humble boatman. Cyreg Paddlehorn is my name. Like most of the Paddlehorns before me, I make my living by tacking the swamps of Mort Myre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Hey. What're you doin' here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come to help the Myreque. I've brought weapons.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: It don't look like you have suitable weapons to me! Get out of here you fake!

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, I see ya got some weapons. But here's da thing. These guys out here, they don't wanna be found. They paid me good money to keep em hidden.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Now, you seems all sincered up to me... It's choking me up right here. But hey, I's godda do my job or da kids don't get fed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I just want to deliver these weapons.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, I's got an idea. Say I asks you a few questions and you answer them all correct and so on... Well that'd make me trust you's. How's dat sound?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds fine to me... Go ahead, shoot!

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Hey... Don't tempt me! You's dicing wiv death here my friend!

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, [first/second/third and final] question. Name the only female member of the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sani Piliu.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Santi Peliou.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sani Peridou.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sandi Pherimou.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, [first/second/third and final] question. Who is the youngest member of the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sani Piliu.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ivan Strom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Veliaf Hurtz.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Radigad Ponfit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, [first/second/third and final] question. Who is the leader of the Myreque in Mort Myre?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sani Piliu.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Harold Evans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Veliaf Hurtz.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Radigad Ponfit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, [first/second/third and final] question. Which member of the Myreque was originally a scholar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sani Piliu.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Harold Evans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Radigad Ponfit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Polmafi Ferdygris.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, [first/second/third and final] question. What is the name of the boatman who helps the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Geof Paddleman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cyreg Paddlebone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gyrec Paddlehorn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cyreg Paddlehorn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, [first/second/third and final] question. What vampyre family rules over Morytania?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Drunken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Drakan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Draynor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Praymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, interesting answer. That's the first question done.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: An interesting response. That's the second question answered.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Hmm. Very interesting. That's the third question answered.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: I'm sorry but you got no questions correct! Go on get outta here...

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: I'm sorry but you only got one question correct! Go on get outta here...

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: I'm sorry but you only got two questions correct! Go on get outta here...

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, I believes ya... You can go on in. Head through the doors behind the tree. Once you's in, take the first cave on the right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have to answer to you!

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: You do if you know what's good for you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No reason!

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Come on... Speak up! I may be able to help you. However, yous gotta know that your tresspassing and that by rights I should slap your around a bit. You know, just like as a warning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm lost. Can you show me the way out?

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: You got yourself here... Now get yourself out... Quickly if I were you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to go.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Sure... Be on your way stranger.

sections.title: Talking to Curpile Fyod again before convincing him

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Back again? You ain't getting in 'till you answer my questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well let's hear them then.

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Okay, first question...

sections.title: Talking to Curpile Fyod again after convincing him

lines.speaker: Curpile Fyod
lines.text: Wat ya still doing out here? Head through the doors behind the tree. Once you's in, take the first cave on the right.

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: Hey! Who are you and why are you here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay. I'm a friend. I'm here to deliver some weapons to you.

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: Weapons? You'd better talk to Veliaf then. I'll be keeping an eye on you though, so don't go trying anything.

sections.title: Speaking to Myreque members before speaking to Veliaf

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: I'm keeping my eye on you. Nothing personal. We just need to be careful with newcomers. Anyway, Veliaf will want to speak to you.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Greetings! Good to see some new blood. You'd better speak with Veliaf. He's in charge here.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Hello there. Here to help the cause are you? Speak to Veliaf. He'll sort you out.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: Hi there. You're new here aren't you? You'd best go and talk with Veliaf.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Oh... Hello. Sorry, but I'm not sure if I'm supposed to be talking to you. You'd better speak to Veliaf.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf before speaking to all other myreque members

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Ah, well met. We'll discuss those weapons shortly. I need to finish something first though. Feel free to introduce yourself to the others in the meantime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You... I saw you come in. Harold didn't kill you though so I'm guessing you're no enemy. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm [Player name]. Don't worry, I'm a friend. I was asked to bring you some weapons.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: It's true that we could do with some supplies. Thank you for bringing them here. I'll have a proper look over them in a moment but there's something I must finish first.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Feel free to introduce yourself to the others in the meantime.

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: Hello there. Forgive me if I seem cautious of you. We just need to be careful with newcomers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's fine. Do you mind if I ask a few questions?

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: Hello there. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to ask a few questions if that's okay?

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: Sure. I'll do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your job here?

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: Cheating death seems to be my main role. I'm the main fighter for the group so I'm always in the thick of it. It's gone okay for me so far though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about this group?

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: We're a good team, though many of the others haven't had any proper training before. I used to be in a militia so I've been trying to share my knowledge as best I can.

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: In most cases we end up having to improvise though. Thankfully, this lot are good at that. Ivan over there, he saved our bacon in a recent run in with some juves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Juves? What are they?

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: Juveniles, the lowest form of vampyre. We come across them quite often around here. They may be the weakest vampyres but they're still very dangerous.

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: They're stronger and faster than many well trained soldiers, and twice as bloodthirsty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your grasp of the situation?

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: The situation is grave. It would be dangerous to think differently.

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: We're really up against it, but we have no choice. This is a battle for our very existence and we need to employ any tactics to achieve our goal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you heard any rumours?

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: Yes, I hear them all the time. I don't pay any attention to them though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Ah, I see you've introduced yourself to Veliaf. Are you planning to join the fray against the Drakans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe. I'd like to ask a few questions if that's okay?

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Hello again! How can I be of service to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to ask you few questions.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Sure, go ahead. I'll answer as best I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your job here?

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: I help with anything I can. I used to be a mercenary before joining the Myreque so I know my way around a blade. I've relied on Veliaf's sword arm many a time. I like to think that he can rely on mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: We're the only resistance against the vampyres. We're seriously outnumbered though. Veliaf doesn't like to hear us saying 'hopeless' but that's our situation at the minute.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What're your plans for the future?

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: That's more a question for Veliaf. Ultimately though, our goal is to rid Morytania of the vampyres so that we might reclaim our right to exist.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: For me personally, I hope that more people will join our ranks soon. Strengh comes in numbers after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you heard any rumours?

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: I have. Something you might find interesting is that Safalaan Hallow, the leader of another Myreque group, has apparently managed to sneak one of his people into the vampyre capital, Darkmeyer.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: I'm not sure how true that is. It would be great though!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Ah, I see you've introduced yourself to Veliaf. Now, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you mind if I ask a few questions?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Salutations! How are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine thanks. I'd like to ask a few questions if that's okay?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Not at all. I'm sure I'll be able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: I'm a key advisor to Veliaf. He seeks my council on many issues before we engage the enemy. Not that we do that very often. Our foes heavily outnumber us.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: We don't give up though. After all, all that is required for evil to survive is for good people to do nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Well there's multiple groups of us. Even we don't know exactly how many. The less we know about our allies, the less we can reveal to the vampyres if captured.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Veliaf leads this group. We operate throughout Mort Myre and the surrounding villages. He reports to Calsidiu who is the head of our resistance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about Morytania?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: All the land on this side of the Salve is known as Morytania. Humans and vampyres are the main occupants of the region, though other races also live out here.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: The humans here live like cattle with the vampyres taking regular blood tithes to keep themselves fed. All in all, it's a dire place. Though we hope to change that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you heard any rumours?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Yes, I hear them all the time. They're neither correct or even all that interesting though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: Hello. Very nice to meet you! The name's Sani Piliu. What's yours?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm [Player name]. Nice to meet you Sani.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: The pleasure's all mine! I've got a good feeling about you. I reckon you're going to do great things for us! You have a powerful aura. Sorry, that must seem really strange.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, perhaps a little. It's nice of you to say so in any case though. I'd like to ask a few questions if that's okay?

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: Oh, hello [Player name]. Nice to see you again! How's things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine thanks. Do you mind if I ask a few questions?

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: Sure, go ahead. I'll answer as best I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me a bit about yourself.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: There's not much to tell really. I was nine years old when my family was ripped to pieces by a vampyre.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: One moment I was a happy member of a loving family, the next I was an orphan living a hand to mouth existence.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: Somehow I managed to get by, eking out a living wherever I could. People took pity on me and I earnt food by helping them out.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: I quickly found I had a very useful set of skills and there were plenty of people willing to pay for them.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: I bumped into Veliaf in Canifis. He saw that I would be an asset to the Myreque and invited me to join. I said yes of course. I couldn't turn down the chance to fight against those who took away my family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: Well Veliaf is the leader of our group. We're not the only ones in Morytania though. Veliaf reports to someone called Calsidiu who's in charge of all the Myreque. I've never met any of the others though.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: Our hope is to one day bring an end to the reign of Lowerniel Drakan and his vampyres.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: For now though, we're just trying to find any weaknesses we can take advantage of. We need to know how to kill them before we take the war to them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You make it sound easy!

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: With determination you can achieve great things. However, Veliaf always tells us not to underestimate the enemy. I've made that mistake before. I won't be making it again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on in Morytania?

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: The entire region is ruled over by Lowerniel Drakan and his family. Everyone living here must pay blood tithes to the vampyres. The tithes keep the vampyres fed and ensure that people are too weak to oppose them.

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: That's where we come in. We won't just offer our family members to the Drakans as a weekly snack. We're going to fight back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this your main camp?

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: It's our only camp! At least, for our group. I know there's more Myreque in other parts of Morytania but I don't know exactly where.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there. Veliaf said that I should introduce myself to everyone.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Okay. Well my name is Ivan. It's nice to meet you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you mind if I asked you a few questions?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Oh, hello again. How are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine thanks. Would you mind if I asked you a few questions?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: That's fine. I don't know how much I can help though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Me personally? I shouldn't be here really. I'm training to be a priest and I was meant to travel to Paterdomus to learn from Drezel. The trip's too dangerous at the moment though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it really important for you to train as a priest? I'd have thought there'd be more pressing issues.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Veliaf thinks it would be useful to have a priest to advise him. I think he also wants me further away from harm until I'm more experienced.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: We're the only ones brave enough to put up a fight against the Drakans. I'm proud to be one of them.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: I do worry that I might let the others down though. I'm not as skilled as they are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about Morytania?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: It's a lost land. The evil Drakan family rule all of the region. People cling to each other in fear when the blood tithes are to be paid. It's horrible!

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: It won't be like this forever though. The Myreque will bring light back to this dark place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you heard any rumours?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Sorry, but I haven't heard anything notable recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Speaking to Veliaf after introduced to other members

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met. I see you've introduced yourself to everyone. Shall we discuss those weapons now? Or was there something else you wanted to talk about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about the weapons.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Let's see what you have for us.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: These weapons look great! Many thanks.

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Vampyre!

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: It's Vanstrom!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... What?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Well done adventurer! You led me right to them. The little dears are going to wonder which side you're on!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: The Drakans have been wanting you lot dealt with for a while now. We wouldn't want to disappoint them, would we?

lines.speaker: Sani Piliu
lines.text: We're dead!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Quite right.

lines.speaker: Harold Evans
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Sorry Harold, you too!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: And now... A little gift for the rest of you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Ah. I'm afraid you don't have suitable weapons for us. Sorry friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What weapons do you need?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well we all have our own specialisms. We could do with a longsword, two shortswords, a dagger, a mace and a warhammer, all made from steel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll come back once I have them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're the leader of this group?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That's right. I can't say I particularly asked for it. The others look up to me though, so they decided I was the best man for the job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about your organisation?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well I wouldn't say we're an organisation. We're really not that organised.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Anyway though, we're a group of Myreque freedom fighters. We're dedicated to mounting a resistance against the Drakan family and their vampyre kin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Morytania?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: If legend is to be believed, it was once a beautiful place. But then Lowerniel Drakan and his vampyres arrived.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is Lowerniel Drakan?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Lowerniel Vergidiyad Drakan is his full name. He's the vampyre that heads up the Drakan family. Helped by his brother Ranis and his sister Vanescula, he rules over all of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You sound very bitter.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: So would you if you lost everyone dear to you at the hands of such monsters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are your plans for the future?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: At the moment we're maintaining a low profile here at our hideout. We were recently spotted while out on a scouting mission so we're playing it safe and lying low.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Looking further ahead, we need to build more support if we have any hope of taking on the vampyres. We also need to learn more about their weaknesses, so we can take advantage of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Sani... Harold... That murderer killed them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm so sorry. This is all my fault.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I don't understand though. He said you led him here. How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He was the one that asked me to bring the weapons to you. He looked like a human though.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I'm sorry friend, but he clearly took advantage of you. Vampyres can shapeshift to look like humans. Someone from around here might have realised. You were not to know though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. I'm still responsible though.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The burden of what happened here is for your conscience to bear. That said, you managed to kill that pet of his. We might all be dead if not for you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I imagine you now see why we fight for our freedom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I think I understand. Is there anything I can do to help now? I first met Vanstrom in Canifis. I could go back and see if he's still there?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I suspect he will have moved on now. You're welcome to try though. I know a shortcut that you might find useful.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: If you head back into the main cave and go north you'll reach a wall. There's a hidden door in that wall that leads right into the basement of the Hair of the Dog Tavern.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's very useful. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Once you are done, I'd appreciate you coming back here to discuss a few things. You held your own against that creature. We could use someone like you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to join your organisation.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well we could definitely use someone like you. You should go and see if Vanstrom is still in Canifis though. Come back once you're done, and we'll talk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you plan to do now?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We won't be able to stay here now that Vanstrom knows where we've been hiding. We have some time though. He'll expect that we're all dead. We'll use that time to form a plan of action.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get out of here again?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: If you head back into the main cave and go north you'll reach a wall. There's a hidden door in that wall that leads right into the basement of the Hair of the Dog Tavern.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: That monster! He killed them! We'll make them all pay!

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: This is a dark day for the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm so sorry for your loss.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Don't worry. No one blames you. You were tricked and that's that. It serves no useful purpose to blame any of ourselves for this.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Thank Saradomin you managed to kill that awful beast! I just wish we could have done more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm so sorry for your loss. If there's anything I can do, please let me know.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: I will. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Stranger
lines.text: Hello friend. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry. I thought you were someone else.

lines.speaker: Stranger
lines.text: Who did you think I was?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you were that dirty murderer Vanstrom.

lines.speaker: Stranger
lines.text: Woah! It sounds like you've got a score to settle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I definitely have!
